title: Moral Self-correction is Not An Innate Capability in Language Models
main_finding: Moral self-correction is not an inherent capability of LLMs, as they are neither morally sensitive nor able to effectively incorporate external feedback during the self-correction process.

types_of_self_correction.name: Intrinsic self-correction
types_of_self_correction.description: Relies solely on prompts that specify the desired objective of outputs

types_of_self_correction.name: Extrinsic self-correction
types_of_self_correction.description: Uses external feedback from humans or stronger LLMs to detect flaws in responses and improve model outputs

warning: The paper contains offensive and upsetting language in examples
